Beltrán-Leyva Organization.description: Mexican drug cartel founded in 1996 by the five Beltrán Leyva brothers, originally part of the Sinaloa Cartel. It trafficked cocaine, heroin, and marijuana, and engaged in human smuggling, money laundering, extortion, kidnapping, and murder. The cartel infiltrated Mexican government agencies and Interpol. It was declared disbanded in 2011 after the arrest of its last major lieutenant, though remnants continued fighting until 2017.

It consists of approximately 450 members and operates primarily in San Bernardino, Orange, Riverside, and Los Angeles counties. The gang is heavily involved in murder, drug trafficking, arms trafficking, and other violent crimes, and has deep ties to the Mexican Mafia.

Live Oak Boys.description: Irish-American street gang based in New Orleans, formed in 1858 by 'Red' Bill Wilson. Named after their oaken clubs, they dominated the city during the 1860s and 1870s, engaging in assault, arson, and vandalism for hire. Internal conflicts and law enforcement pressure led to their decline by 1880.

Ashok Mahto gang.description: Indian criminal gang active in Bihar, led by Ashok Mahto and Pintu Mahto. Responsible for the 2005 killing of MP Rajo Singh and numerous caste-related murders in the late 1990s. Engaged in a long-running rivalry with the Akhilesh Singh gang, rooted in caste conflict between Koeris and Bhumihars. The gang operated primarily in Nawada, Nalanda, and Sheikhpura districts.

descriptions: The two gangs were engaged in a violent rivalry over caste conflict and control of resources.

Loners Motorcycle Club.description: International outlaw motorcycle club founded in Woodbridge, Ontario, Canada in 1979 by Frank Lenti and Gennaro Raso. Operates chapters in Canada, Italy, the United States, and other countries. Known for its elaborate patch and involvement in conflicts with other biker gangs. Estimated to have 1,000 active members globally.

Lone Legion Brotherhood.description: An outlaw motorcycle club based in Blenheim, New Zealand, established in the 1970s. It controlled the town as its exclusive territory until around 2007. The club was involved in a fatal shooting in 2007 and later demolished its clubhouse. In 2019, it patched over to the Outlaws MC.

DarkMarket.description: DarkMarket was an English-language cybercrime forum created by Renukanth Subramaniam in London. It facilitated the buying and selling of stolen identities and credit card data, with 2,500 users at its peak. The forum was infiltrated by FBI agent J. Keith Mularski, leading to over 60 arrests worldwide. It was shut down in 2008, and Subramaniam was sentenced to nearly five years in prison in 2010.

Società foggiana.description: The Società foggiana is a mafia-type criminal cartel operating in Foggia and its province, with infiltrations in other Italian regions. It emerged in the early 1980s as a splinter of Raffaele Cutolo's Nuova Camorra Organizzata and gained recognition in 1994. Known for its brutality, it lacks a single leader but is organized into family-based 'batteries' with a federal structure. It is considered one of the most violent Italian mafias.

descriptions: Società foggiana emerged after the murder of an SCU lieutenant, indicating a split or rivalry.
edges.evidence_quote: The murder of Giuseppe Laviano, lieutenant of the Sacra Corona Unita in Foggia (whose body was never found), in January 1989, marked a turning point in the mafia war and the rise of Rocco Moretti, known as il porco (the pig), in the context of the Foggia organized crime at the expense of Laviano himself. After the murder, Moretti became the head of the new criminal organization Società foggiana in Foggia.
